zh.lifeSummary: 當María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla於1264年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德時，她的父親Alfonso De Molina62歲，母親Mayor Alfonso de Meneses34歲。她和Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz至少有5個兒子和4個女兒。她在西班牙被列為 	 señora de molina, señora de ucero, princesa del reino de león, reina consorte de castilla y león de 1284 a 1285 y luego reina regente hasta la mayoría de edad de su hijo fernando iv., regenta de castilla y león, reina de castilla y león。她去世於1321年7月1日在她的家鄉享年57歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德。

nameMeanings.name: Alfonso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Mario, Carlos, Jorge, Pedro, Ramon, Luis, Rafael, Jesus, Gustavo.  Italian Antonio, Salvatore, Elio, Heriberto, Leonardo, Lorenzo, Aldo, Angelo, Annamaria, Antonino, Carmelo, Carmine.
Spanish and southern Italian: from the personal name Alfonso, derived from the Visigothic name Adefonsus, formed from Gothic hathu ‘war’ + funs ‘ready’. Compare Alphonso .

variants.name: Alfonsi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Ferdinando, Antonio, Benigno, Cesare, Elisabetta, Giuseppe, Italo, Marco, Mario, Mirella, Silvio, Tiberio.
Italian (southern): patronymic or plural form of   Alfonso  .

variants.name: D'Alonzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Augustino, Domenic, Emelio, Fiore, Rocco, Romeo.
Italian (D'Alonzo): patronymic from Alonso, avariant of the personal name Alfonso .

variants.name: De Fronzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Rocco, Angelo, Biagio, Saverio.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Fronzo, a shortform of Alfonso .

variants.name: Alphonso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Andres, Belmiro, Eulalia, Gaspar, Manuel.  French Celestine, Marcel, Pierre.
Altered form of Spanish and southern Italian   Alfonso  .

variants.name: Alonso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Jorge, Francisco, Pedro, Luis, Roberto, Angel, Enrique, Jesus, Antonio, Leonardo, Cecilio, Dario, Marco, Angelo, Clemente, Elio, Heriberto, Ciriaco, Delio.
Spanish: from the personal name Alonso, the Castilian variant of Alfonso .

variants.name: Di Fonzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Alfonso, Biagio, Carmine, Domenic, Eliseo, Eraldo, Onofrio, Panfilo, Pio, Raffaele, Renato, Salvatore, Valentino.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Fonzo, a shortform of Alfonso . The change of s to z is typicalof southern and central Italy.

variants.name: Afonso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Antonio, Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Domingos, Ana, Mario, Abilio, Alfredo, Americo, Arlindo, Francisco, Sergio.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Caetano, Lidio.
Portuguese: from the personal name Afonso, an equivalent of Spanish Alfonso .

variants.name: Alphonse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Marcel, Raymonde, Antoine, Benoit, Clovis, Hermite, Jacques, Michel, Pierre, Ricot, Yves.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Seychellois: from the French personal name Alphonse, from Alphonsus, a Latinized spelling of Spanish Alfonso .

variants.name: Alfonzo
meanings: Spanish: variant of   Alfonso  .

variants.name: D'Alfonso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Alfonso, Claudio, Ermanno, Sal, Angelo, Carmel, Delio, Domenic, Domenico, Fiore, Mauro, Nunzi, Nunzio, Pasquale.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name   Alfonso  ; thisform is typical of Abruzzo.

nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Molina
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Ana, Roberto.  Portuguese Joao, Margarida.

Spanish, Catalan, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from any of numerous places called Molina, in particular the one in Guadalajara province. This surname is also found in France (compare   Moline  ). For certain Jewish families, this is an adoption of the Iberian surname at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Moline
meanings: Americanized form of Swedish   Molin  .
Catalan (Moliné): occupational name for a miller, from moliner miller. This surname is also found in southern France.
French and Jewish (from France): altered form of   Molina  , a surname of Spanish and Catalan origin.

nameMeanings.name: Meneses
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jorge, Francisco, Mario, Juan, Orlando, Alejandro, Arturo, Benigno.  Portuguese Joao, Mateus, Henrique, Joaquim, Ligia.
Spanish and Portuguese: habitational name from either of two places called Meneses de Campos in Palencia and Castile, Spain.

variants.name: Menezes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luiz, Marcos, Sergio, Alberto, Antero, Eduardo, Adelino, Alda, Ana, Ana Maria.  Portuguese Ademir, Almir, Guilherme, Marcio, Paulo.  Indian Geetha, Jeevan, Naresh, Rahul, Rohit, Shalini, Vinod, Vivek.
Portuguese and Spanish: variant of   Meneses  . This surname is also found in western India and in East Timor, to which it was brought by Portuguese colonists in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: María
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Maria
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: “La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of   Olaf  , also widely used in southwest Sweden.

nameMeanings.name: Grande”
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Rocco, Domenic, Mario, Antonio, Santo, Orlando, Americo, Carmine, Gennaro, Luigi, Pasquale, Saverio, Alberico.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Alicia, Ricardo, Jorge, Juan, Raul, Angel, Atilio.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: nickname for someone of large stature, in either a literal or figurative sense, from grande ‘tall, large’.
German: habitational name from Grande in Holstein or Grand in Bavaria.

variants.name: Grant
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, common in Scotland, where it is the name of a famous clan. It is derived from a nickname meaning ‘large’ (Anglo-Norman grand). In the United States the name is sometimes bestowed in honour of the Civil War general and 18th president, Ulysses S. Grant ( 1822–85 ).

variants.name: Grandi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Elio, Dante, Dino, Geno, Pietro.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Grande  .

variants.name: De Grandis
meanings: Italian (rarely Degrandis): patronymic from Grande . It is formed with the Latin ablative plural suffix -is imparting the broader sense ‘belonging to, of’, in this case ‘member of the Grandi family’ (compare Grandi ).

variants.name: Del Grande
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dino, Angelo, Domenic, Marco, Oreste, Reno.
Italian: literally ‘of or belonging to the big one or the great one’ (see   Grande  ), hence a name denoting the son, apprentice, associate, or servant of such a man.

variants.name: Grannemann
meanings: German: perhaps a variant of Grandemann, itself a variant of Grande .

variants.name: Grandinetti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Aldo, Antonio, Carmin, Domenic, Guido, Martino.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Grandinetto, a diminutive of Grande .

variants.name: De Grande
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Romain.
Italian: patronymic from the nickname   Grande   ‘tall, large’.
Flemish (also Degrande): from French and Walloon Legrand ‘the tall, the large’. Compare De Groote .

en.lifeSummary: When María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla was born in 1264, in Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spain, her father, Alfonso De Molina, was 62 and her mother, Mayor Alfonso de Meneses, was 34. She had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz. Her occupation is listed as  	 señora de molina, señora de ucero, princesa del reino de león, reina consorte de castilla y león de 1284 a 1285 y luego reina regente hasta la mayoría de edad de su hijo fernando iv., regenta de castilla y león, reina de castilla y león in Spain. She died on 1 July 1321, in her hometown, at the age of 57, and was buried in Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spain.

Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla est née en 1264, à Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Alfonso De Molina, avait 62 ans et sa mère, Mayor Alfonso de Meneses, avait 34 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz. La profession de sa indiquée est  	 señora de molina, señora de ucero, princesa del reino de león, reina consorte de castilla y león de 1284 a 1285 y luego reina regente hasta la mayoría de edad de su hijo fernando iv., regenta de castilla y león, reina de castilla y león à Espagne. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla nasceu em 1264, em Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Alfonso De Molina, tinha 62 anos e sua mãe, Mayor Alfonso de Meneses, tinha 34 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas com Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz. Sua profissão aparece como  	 señora de molina, señora de ucero, princesa del reino de león, reina consorte de castilla y león de 1284 a 1285 y luego reina regente hasta la mayoría de edad de su hijo fernando iv., regenta de castilla y león, reina de castilla y león em Espanha. Ela faleceu em 1 de julho de 1321, em sua cidade natal, com 57 anos, e foi sepultada em Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla родилась в 1264 г. в местности валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания, её отцу по имени Alfonso De Molina было 62 года, а матери, которую звали Mayor Alfonso de Meneses, 34. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz.  Она работала по профессии  	 señora de molina, señora de ucero, princesa del reino de león, reina consorte de castilla y león de 1284 a 1285 y luego reina regente hasta la mayoría de edad de su hijo fernando iv., regenta de castilla y león, reina de castilla y león в местности Испания. Она умерла 1 июля 1321 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания.
